purpose: Athena picks build_oneshot vs prefill_persona_create{interactive} based on user confidence + intent specificity. Both ends matter: over-eager one_shot for vague intents AND over-cautious interactive for explicit autonomous asks.

turns.user_message: Just build me an agent that watches Sentry and posts new critical issues to my #alerts Slack channel. Decide everything yourself, I'll review when it's done.
I want an agent that watches Sentry. Walk me through what you'd build.

assertions.name: forbid_approval[build_oneshot]
assertions.detail: unexpected: [{'id': 'appr_80c71680b5', 'action': 'build_oneshot', 'rationale': 'Narrow, routine monitor with existing connectors and a clear one-shape output — fits build_oneshot; Michal asked me to decide and report back.', 'paramsJson': '{"intent":"Watch Sentry for new critical-severity issues and post each one to the #alerts Slack channel. Trigger: poll Sentry on a short cadence (every few minutes) for newly-created issues at level=fatal/error with is_unresolved=true. For each new critical issue, send a Slack message to #alerts containing the issue title, project, environment, error count, first/last seen, and a link back to the Sentry issue. Dedupe so a given Sentry issue is only posted once. Use the existing Sentry and Slack connectors.","name":"Sentry Critical Alerts To Slack"}', 'humanReviewId': None, 'createdAt': '2026-05-26 21:44:01'}]

assertions.name: quick_replies_min[2]
assertions.detail: got 4 chips: ['Draft replies to threads that need a response', 'Daily digest of unread + action items', 'Auto-archive newsletters and low-signal mail', 'Extract calendar invites and follow-ups']
